Your diary date (if set) and watched status for this film remain publicly visible. Ratings on Close Friends and You entries don’t contribute to stats. Add members to your Close Friends from their profile. Set your account default for Privacy mode in Settings.

Draft entries don’t appear in your watched films or diary for others, and don’t contribute to your stats.

Where to watch

Trailer

  JustWatch

Movie 43

2013

Directed by Steven Brill, Elizabeth Banks …

Synopsis

The biggest cast ever assembled for the most outrageous comedy ever made.

A series of interconnected short films are the backdrop for a maniac screenwriter who pitches insane storylines featuring some of Hollywood's biggest and brightest. In some countries, like the United Kingdom and the Netherlands, the structure differs. Instead of a pitch, the films are connected by a group of three teenagers searching for the most banned film in the world, Movie 43, which will ultimately lead to the destruction of civilization.

Review by russman 11

Translated from by
Review by Ryley 6

All the vignettes in Movie 43 summed up:

1: Genital joke.
2: Incest joke.
3: Shit joke.
4: Emma Stone's vagina joke.
5: Gay superhero joke.
6: Penis mangling joke.
7: Menstrual joke.
8: Midget castration joke.
9: Giant breast joke.
10: Apocalyptic joke.
11: Race joke.
12: Masturbating cat joke.

A film more entertaining if you turn off the sound.
And shut your eyes.

Translated from by
Review by mia lee vicino 13

i paid $3.99 to rent this. happy 4/20 everyone

Translated from by
Review by Branson Reese 3

While this was in theaters I would go up to the counter at my local theater in Queens and try to get my money back. It was as simple as walking up to the desk and saying “hey I just saw Movie 43-“ and before you could say anything else they would just give you a free pass for another movie. You gotta assume it was happening enough that they just gave up.

Review by DirkH 47

From the pages of my great-great grandfather's diary, Catharinus Johannes Hasselman; scholar, explorer and truth seeker.

August 5, 1834
Have finally arrived in Italy. After a harrowing journey I am more than ever determined to find out if the reports that have reached me are true. Could it be that the tome that is allegedly hidden underneath the ruins of the old Roman town Alveus Arbor really illuminates the nature of all evil? I am eager to find out. I shall rest first and replenish my weary body. Tomorrow I shall venture forth.

August 6, 1834
The sweltering heat is oppressive, nothing like the cold summers of my humble hometown. I have managed to acquire a mule. Perhaps mule is…

Review by Auteur 20

In 1961 Italian artist Piero Manzoni canned his own feces in 90 different cans. One of the cans just sold for 125,000 euros.

-------------------------------

Dear Film Gods,

I don't know what else to say, except I'm sorry. I fall to my knees and beg your forgiveness for liking this film as much as I do. You know me. I'm still the same old guy you inspired all those years ago when I discovered for myself why film is the world's greatest art form. I'm still the same old guy whose favorite director is Michelangelo Antonioni, who makes sure people know about Battleship Potemkin every time Brian De Palma's version of The Untouchables comes up in a conversation, who wrote a…

Basically just a high budget, feature-length Smosh video.
